mission.title: backstage: [RFC] Ability to use the signed in users oauth credentials in the Scaffolder
mission.description: [RFC] Ability to use the signed in users oauth credentials in the Scaffolder. Requested by 13+ users.

steps.title: Check current backstage setup
steps.description:
Verify your backstage version and configuration:
```bash
npx backstage-cli info
```
This feature requires a working backstage installation.

steps.title: Review backstage configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant backstage configuration:
**Status:** Open for comments

## Need
Using credentials from a Personal Access Token that is a god token, or Github Apps is some times not enough security and Integrators would like to have the ability to grab a token from the users signed in oauth

steps.title: Apply the fix for [RFC] Ability to use the signed in users oauth credentials…
steps.description:
## Hey, I just made a Pull Request!

Reason for the change: we want to rely on the user's permission which is more granular and traceable compared to using the GitHub app.

We are introducing a change to the Scaffolder, we want to use the GitHub personal access token to create and push to the new repository. 

The old behaviour would use the GitHub app credentials and the "Scaffolder" author, with this change we would check if the GitHub OAuth is enabled and prompt the user to Login with

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "[RFC] Ability to use the signed in users oauth credentials…" is functioning correctly.

prerequisites.description: A working backstage installation or development environment.
